name: University of Environment and Sustainable Development (UESD)

faculty_name: School of Natural and Environmental Sciences (SNES)

fees: Ghanaian Students: Approx. GHC 2,000 per semester
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (Best six WASSCE subjects)
mode: Fieldwork, laboratory practicals and research projects
entry_requirements: WASSCE: Credit passes (A1-C6) in core English, core Mathematics, Integrated Science/Social Studies
Three (3) relevant elective subjects, including at least one of: Economics, Geography, Mathematics, Biology, Chemistry, Physics or General Agriculture
Aggregate 24 or better
SSSCE/GBCE holders: Aggregate 36 or better with equivalent credits
level_note: Offered under the School of Natural and Environmental Sciences (SNES), one of UESD's two operational schools
Strong focus on fieldwork, laboratory research and hands-on environmental science training

fees: Ghanaian Students: Approx. GHC 3,800 per semester
cutt_off: 2025: Aggregate 24 or better (Best six WASSCE subjects)
entry_requirements: A good first degree (minimum Second Class Lower) in Aquaculture, Fisheries Science, Biology or a related field or a related discipline from an accredited university
Official transcripts and two academic/professional references
Research proposal relevant to the programme area
Interview may be required
level_note: Offered under the School of Graduate Studies at UESD's Somanya campus
Research-focused programme supervised by faculty with expertise in environment and sustainability
